questions.question: How do I develop self-awareness if I've never really thought about it before?
questions.answer: Start with structured journaling. Every evening, write down: (1) What emotion did I feel most strongly today? (2) What triggered that emotion? (3) How did I respond? What would I do differently? After two weeks, you'll start seeing patterns. Also, ask three trusted people: 'What's one thing I do that I might not realize?' Their answers will surprise you and reveal blind spots.

questions.question: What's the difference between self-awareness and being self-absorbed?
questions.answer: Self-awareness is about understanding yourself to improve your relationships and performance. Self-absorption is about being consumed with yourself without regard for others. Self-aware people notice how their behavior affects others and adjust. Self-absorbed people only think about their own feelings and needs. The key difference: self-awareness leads to better connections; self-absorption leads to isolation.

questions.question: Can you give an example of a blind spot and how to address it?
questions.answer: A common blind spot: thinking you're a good listener when you actually interrupt people. You might believe you're being helpful by jumping in with solutions, but others experience you as dismissive. To address it: (1) Ask a colleague for honest feedback, (2) Record yourself in a meeting and watch it back, (3) Practice the '5-second rule' — wait 5 seconds after someone finishes speaking before responding. This creates space to truly hear them.

questions.question: How does self-awareness help with career growth?
questions.answer: Self-aware people make better career decisions because they know their values and strengths. They choose roles that align with who they are, not just what pays well. They also build stronger relationships because they understand how others perceive them. In interviews, they can honestly articulate their strengths and weaknesses. In leadership roles, they inspire trust because they're authentic and transparent.

questions.question: What's the biggest obstacle to becoming more self-aware?
questions.answer: The biggest obstacle is the illusion of self-awareness. Research shows that 95% of people think they're self-aware, but only 10-15% actually are. We're naturally biased toward seeing ourselves positively. To overcome this: (1) Seek feedback regularly and actually listen to it, (2) Notice when you feel defensive — that's usually where your blind spots are, (3) Work with a coach or therapist who can provide objective perspective.
